name: The trails under the pines — loop from the road of La Lette Blanche
name_original: Les pistes sous les pins — boucle au départ de la route de La Lette Blanche
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vielle-Saint-Girons offers routes through diverse landscapes, including extensive pine forests and 17 kilometers of Atlantic coastline. The region features a network of cycle paths, such as those integrated into the Vélodyssée, connecting to towns like Mimizan and Capbreton. Inland, routes often pass by Lac de Léon and the Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Vielle-Saint-Girons The most popular touring cycling route is The trails under the pines — loop from the road of La Lette Blanche, a 42.2 miles (67.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through shaded pine forests. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Coastal Trail – Lespecier Trail loop from Étang de Léon, a moderate 48.8 miles (78.6 km) path. This route combines coastal sections with views of Étang de Léon. Local touring cyclists also love the Vieux-Boucau Marine Lake – Soustons Stream loop from Étang de Léon, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail leading through areas around the lake and stream, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Vielle-Saint-Girons is defined by pine forests, Atlantic coastal paths, and routes around Lac de Léon. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Vielle-Saint-Girons are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 6000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vielle-Saint-Girons's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: Vielle-Saint-Girons offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 70 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy forest paths to more challenging coastal rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the cycling routes around Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: The region's cycling routes primarily traverse the sprawling &lt;strong&gt;pine forests&lt;/strong&gt; of the Landes, offering shaded and picturesque environments. You'll also find routes along the &lt;strong&gt;Atlantic coastline&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring diverse beaches, and paths around the serene &lt;strong&gt;Lac de Léon&lt;/strong&gt;. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, characteristic of the Landes region.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Vielle-Saint-Girons is well-suited for family cycling, with many easy paths. Over 50 of the available routes are classified as easy, making them ideal for families. These often follow dedicated cycle paths through the forests or along calmer lake shores, away from heavy traffic.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Vielle-Saint-Girons are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1284145161/the-trails-under-the-pines-loop-from-the-road-of-la-lette-blanche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The trails under the pines — loop from the road of La Lette Blanche&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option that takes you through shaded pine forests.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes often pass by significant natural attractions. You can explore the unique ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, often called the 'Landes Amazon,' or enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang de Moïsan&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3551855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight, offering beautiful lakeside scenery.

Q: Are there options for different cycling abilities, from easy to challenging?
A: Absolutely. Vielle-Saint-Girons offers routes for all abilities. There are over 50 easy routes perfect for beginners or casual rides, around 20 moderate routes for those seeking a bit more distance or varied terrain, and a few difficult routes for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41691903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lespecier Trail – Coastal Trail loop from Vielle-Saint-Girons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Vielle-Saint-Girons is typically from spring to autumn. The pine forests provide welcome shade during warmer months, and the coastal breezes can be refreshing. Spring and early autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Is it possible to rent bikes in Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: Yes, Vielle-Saint-Girons is well-equipped with bike rental services. You can find a variety of bikes, including mountain bikes, hybrid bikes, electric bikes, and even specialized electric fatbikes for sand riding, ensuring you have the right equipment for your adventure.

Q: How can I access the cycling routes using public transport or find parking?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, Vielle-Saint-Girons is generally accessible by car, and parking is available in and around the villages and at popular starting points for routes. Many routes are easily reachable from the town center.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vielle-Saint-Girons are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 900 reviews. Cyclists often praise the extensive network of shaded paths through the pine forests, the beautiful coastal sections, and the well-maintained infrastructure.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, many routes pass through or near villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops. For example, the area around Lac de Léon offers options like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guinguette Whypot&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break during your ride.

Q: What is the Vélodyssée, and how does it relate to Vielle-Saint-Girons?
A: The Vélodyssée is a major long-distance cycling route that runs along the Atlantic coast of France. Vielle-Saint-Girons is an integral part of this route, offering connections to towns like Mimizan to the north and Capbreton to the south. Many local touring routes incorporate sections of the Vélodyssée, providing excellent, well-marked paths.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a greater challenge, Vielle-Saint-Girons offers longer and more demanding routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41684269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port of Capbreton – Capbreton Marina loop from Courant d'Huchet&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 118 km route that provides a significant endurance test and takes you further afield.
